cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the question. The question is about why Babbage failed to continue his research work in 1828. The options are A through D. First, I need to read the passage again to understand the context. The passage says that Babbage was working in London for four years after exhibiting his invention in 1822. Then his health broke down and he had to take a long holiday abroad. When he returned in 1828, he was at the end of his resources. Many bills remained unpaid. His chief assistant and co-workers quarreled with him and left with many expensive tools. For one year no work was done. So
